preprocessing.rationale: CPU-friendly, robust to hardware variation. Amplitude-based processing is the standard for basic HAR activities on UT-HAR. The Hampel filter removes CSI outliers, bandpass retains 0.3-3Hz (covering human motion frequencies), and per-channel z-score normalization stabilizes training.

model.rationale: ResNet-1D with residual blocks provides the strongest CPU-friendly baseline for time-series classification. Residual connections prevent gradient vanishing and enable deeper feature extraction. At 400K params and ~20ms inference, it fits comfortably within the 0.5M/30ms constraints while giving the best chance of reaching 85% accuracy on 5-class WiFi CSI HAR.

ablation_candidates.name: bilstm_arch
ablation_candidates.change: replace ResNet-1D with BiLSTM (200K params, simpler model)

ablation_candidates.name: no_hampel
ablation_candidates.change: remove Hampel filter step, rely on normalization only

ablation_candidates.name: lr_0.001_warmup
ablation_candidates.change: add linear warmup for first 5 epochs then cosine decay

ablation_candidates.name: cm_ratio
ablation_candidates.change: add conjugate multiplication (phase sanitization) before amplitude extraction

literature_support.supports: Dataset baseline, CNN-based methods for CSI HAR
ResNet-style architectures for WiFi sensing tasks

agent_decisions.decision: Selected amplitude_only strategy: extract_amplitude → hampel_filter(k=5,thr=3.0) → bandpass(0.3-3Hz) → per_channel_z_score. CPU cost=low, recommended for basic activities.
Selected ResNet-1D (0.4M params, ~20ms CPU inference). Strongest CPU-friendly baseline for 85% accuracy target. Fits within 0.5M/30ms constraints.
